message:
fix(fuzz): fuzz_mmap MarkStaleInPlace addr poison + Advise re-stamp

Re-fuzz validation after the Protect re-stamp fix surfaced two more
fuzz_mmap harness bugs (zero library bugs):

1. MarkStaleInPlace munmap'd the mapping but left m.addr holding
   the just-freed virtual address. The kernel reuses freed VA
   ranges aggressively — a subsequent Op::Map can land at the same
   addr, and Unmap on the stale slot then succeeds (kernel sees the
   addr as currently mapped). Same fd-reuse pattern as fuzz_socket
   (756dcfb1). Set m.addr = MAP_FAILED after the munmap so any later
   op on the stale slot fails as the harness asserts.

2. Op::Advise with MADV_DONTNEED / MADV_FREE / MADV_REMOVE on a
   private anonymous mapping discards / zeroes the pages, wiping
   the GUARD_BYTE stamps. cleanup's check_guards then read 0 (page-
   fault zero-fill) and asserted "guard head corrupted at ...
   left: 0 / right: 253". Re-stamp guards after a successful
   destructive advise — same pattern as the Protect re-stamp.

Validated: 60s re-fuzz of fuzz_mmap → 0 crashes/timeouts.
